analyst_observations: The skill's main measurable impact was on Eval 1 (create from scratch): with-skill avoided fabricating uid:// values, which the baseline did. Fabricated UIDs could cause collisions in real projects.

Eval 2 and 3 showed no pass-rate difference — Claude already handles these patterns well from training data. The skill adds safety (explicit validation checklist) but doesn't change outcomes for simple cases.
The 'no fabricated UIDs' assertion is the only discriminating assertion across all evals — it's the one thing the skill teaches that base Claude doesn't know.
Token overhead is ~31% higher with skill (reading the 348-line SKILL.md). This is acceptable for a safety-focused skill.
The without-skill baseline also flagged 'missing load_steps' as an error (eval 3), which is debatable for Godot 4.6 where load_steps is deprecated. The skill correctly avoids this false positive.
